input.title: Epithalon (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) increases telomerase activity and telomere length in human fetal fibroblasts in vitro
input.abstract: Human fetal fibroblasts (HFF-1 cell line) were treated with epithalon (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly tetrapeptide) at 0.01, 0.1, and 1 µg/mL for 3 passages (approximately 21 days). Telomerase activity, measured by TRAP assay, increased 1.9-fold (p=0.01) at 0.1 µg/mL relative to untreated controls. Telomere length by quantitative PCR increased by 18% at the highest dose (p=0.03). Cell viability remained >95% across all concentrations as measured by MTT assay. These data demonstrate that epithalon directly stimulates telomerase in a human cell model, consistent with reported anti-ageing properties.
